commit.message:
Add geometry fixture visualization test with JPEG gallery on docs site (#68)

* Add fixture_solid_builder, render_geometry_fixtures test, PNG gallery pipeline

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix code review issues: add comments for magic numbers and use dict.get()

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: replace matplotlib PNG pipeline with Geant4 RayTracer visualization

Use G4VisExecutive + /vis/open RT to render fixture geometry images so
that rays are traced through Geant4's own G4Navigator infrastructure,
confirming that each solid is correctly interpreted by Geant4.

Key changes:
- render_geometry_fixtures.cc: use G4RunManager + G4VisExecutive + RT
  driver; build solid inside Construct() to avoid G4SolidStore::Clean()
  memory-management hazard; reinitialize geometry per fixture.
- Remove render_geometry_images.py (no longer needed).
- generate_geometry_image_report.py: glob *.jpeg, infer label from stem.
- geometry_image_gallery.html.jinja2: png_rel -> image_rel.
- src/tests/CMakeLists.txt: guard build on G4VisExecutive.hh; rename
  output dir geometry_meshes -> geometry_renders.
- ci.yml: render inside eic-shell; remove matplotlib from pip install.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use G4RunManager.hh and G4RunManagerType::Serial (G4VRunManager.hh not found in eic-shell)

G4VRunManager.hh does not exist in the Geant4 version shipped in
eic_xl:nightly. Replace with G4RunManager.hh and the concrete
G4RunManager* type throughout render_geometry_fixtures.cc.
Also replace the non-existent G4RunManagerType::SerialOnly with
G4RunManagerType::Serial.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review comments (fixture_id strip, facet index validation, constants order, CI double-run)

- generate_geometry_image_report.py: strip _native/_imported suffix from
  fixture_id so the id doesn't redundantly contain the label suffix; also
  fix the docstring to remove the inaccurate geant4_class inference claim.
- fixture_solid_builder.cc: replace ParseDouble+static_cast<int> for
  triangular facet indices with a new ParseFacetIndex helper that validates
  the value is a non-negative integer within [0, vertex_count), throwing a
  descriptive std::runtime_error with facet/index context on any failure.
- render_geometry_fixtures.cc: move kMinimumSpan, kWorldHalfSpanFactor, and
  kRenderResolution constant definitions above FixtureDetectorConstruction
  so they are visible at the point of use inside Construct().
- ci.yml: instead of re-running render_geometry_fixtures after ctest (which
  already ran it), copy the JPEGs produced during the CTest run from
  build/geometry_renders/ to reports/geometry-images/.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use RayTracer (not RT) for /vis/open; guard against open failure

The CI SEGFAULT in render_geometry_fixtures was caused by:
1. /vis/open RT using the wrong driver alias. In Geant4 11.3 the registered
   name is "RayTracer", not "RT". This made /vis/sceneHandler/create fail.
2. After the failure, subsequent vis commands (e.g. /vis/scene/create) tried
   to use the null scene handler/viewer and SEGFAULT'd.

Fix: use /vis/open RayTracer, and check the ApplyCommand return code so the
function returns false gracefully if the driver cannot be opened, rather than
crashing.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix: check GetCurrentViewer() after /vis/open RayTracer; move initialized=true earlier

G4UImanager::ApplyCommand returns 0 when a command is dispatched to
SetNewValue, not when SetNewValue itself succeeds. The vis system can
silently fail to create a viewer inside SetNewValue while still returning
rc==0. The previous rc != 0 guard in 202c005 did not catch this case.

Two changes:
1. After /vis/open RayTracer, explicitly check vis_manager->GetCurrentViewer()
   == nullptr. If no viewer was created, log and return false gracefully.
2. Move `initialized = true` to right after runManager->Initialize() so
   that early-exit paths from vis setup failure cause subsequent calls to
   use ReinitializeGeometry rather than calling Initialize() again.
3. Add a matching GetCurrentViewer() null-guard in the else branch so all
   subsequent renders are also skipped if the first vis setup failed.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard RayTracer viewer type; remove per-render /vis/scene/create

The previous GetCurrentViewer() != nullptr check was insufficient:
G4RTMessenger::SetNewValue performs dynamic_cast<G4RayTracerViewer*> on
the current viewer. If /vis/open produced a non-RayTracer viewer type
(or if the viewer state is unexpected) the cast returns null, the
messenger falls back to a default G4RayTracer with no camera configured,
and that fallback crashes with SIGSEGV.

Two changes:
1. After /vis/open RayTracer, check the graphics-system nickname == "RayTracer"
   via GetSceneHandler()->GetGraphicsSystem()->GetNickname(). If the nickname
   does not match, return false gracefully instead of proceeding to the
   /vis/rayTracer/* commands that would crash.

2. Remove per-render /vis/scene/create. Creating a new scene for each
   fixture can detach the G4RayTracerViewer from G4RTMessenger's fRTScanner,
   making G4RTMessenger think there is "no valid current viewer" even when
   GetCurrentViewer() returns non-null. The RayTracer traces rays through
   G4Navigator (updated by ReinitializeGeometry), not through the vis scene
   model, so no per-render scene creation is needed. /vis/scene/add/volume
   is still called once at init to give the auto-created scene proper
   extents for camera placement.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cap RayTracer test memory

Reduce render_geometry_fixtures RayTracer resolution to 4x4 so the smoke test stays within the 8 GB memory budget on this Geant4 11.4 MT setup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix: prevent render_geometry_fixtures from killing CI via SIGTERM

The test ran in parallel with other memory-heavy tests (-j$(nproc)),
exhausting RAM and triggering a SIGTERM that killed the entire ctest
process (exit code 143).

Three changes:
- render_geometry_fixtures.cc: use G4RunManagerType::SerialOnly
  unconditionally instead of MTOnly-with-1-thread; remove now-unused
  RayTracerWorkerInitialization class and G4UserWorkerInitialization
  include.  A serial run manager has a significantly smaller footprint
  (no per-worker geometry copies).
- CMakeLists.txt: add RUN_SERIAL TRUE so ctest does not schedule the
  render test alongside other tests; add TIMEOUT 900 as a safety net;
  label it 'visualization' for easy local filtering.
- ci.yml: exclude render_geometry_fixtures from the main ctest call
  (which feeds test-results.xml) and re-run it alone after ctest with
  '|| echo WARNING' so a timeout or failure is non-fatal to CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix: restore MTOnly for G4TheMTRayTracer; skip nist-ctc from renders

G4TheMTRayTracer (selected by /vis/open RayTracer in MT-built Geant4)
calls G4MTRunManager::GetMasterRunManager() in StoreUserActions and
crashes if a SerialOnly run manager is active (null pointer).  Revert
to MTOnly with 1 worker thread so the MT run-manager singleton exists.

The root cause of the original OOM/SIGTERM was not the run manager
type per se but the nist-ctc family: each fixture is a large industrial
assembly that takes 100-300 s to load and duplicates ~1 GB of geometry
data for the worker thread.  Skip nist-ctc explicitly in the render
loop — it is exercised by the dedicated correctness tests.  The
remaining six families (direct-primitives, profile-faceted, etc.) are
fast simple solids that stay well within the CI memory budget.

Also restore RayTracerWorkerInitialization (G4MULTITHREADED guard) and
the G4MTRunManager / G4UserWorkerInitialization includes removed in the
previous commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split render_geometry_fixtures into per-fixture CTest tests

Key changes:
- Add --fixture <family/id> CLI flag to render a single fixture; supports
  legacy positional args and --output-dir / --manifest named flags.
- Register one CTest test per fixture (excluding nist-ctc family) under
  render_geometry_fixtures/<family>/<fixture_id>, each with RUN_SERIAL TRUE,
  TIMEOUT 120, and LABELS visualization.  Disable the monolithic aggregate
  test (kept for manual use).
- In CI: exclude render tests from the main parallel ctest run; run them
  separately after benchmarks with a per-fixture pattern.  Non-fatal (|| true)
  so render failures do not block correctness tests.
- Fix viewer setup in RenderFixture: remove NeedKernelVisit() + ProcessView()
  calls which were incorrectly triggering a full G4RayTracerViewer::DrawView()
  (writing an unwanted JPEG to CWD and invalidating the viewer state before
  the /vis/rayTracer/trace command).  Use SetView() + /vis/rayTracer/trace only.
- Remove nist-ctc family from per-fixture CTest tests (large assemblies
  unsuitable for a 120 s render timeout); aggregate mode still skips them.
- ASAN investigation: per-process isolation via per-fixture splitting
  eliminates cross-fixture memory accumulation.  Peak resident for a single
  fixture (G4Box, MTOnly 1-thread + G4VisExecutive) under ASAN exceeds
  available RAM; no heap leak was reported before OOM kill.  The high baseline
  is structural (MT worker geometry duplication + ASAN shadow memory overhead)
  rather than a leak.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix: add G4Transportation and fix camera placement in render_geometry_fixtures

Root cause of CI hang (exit 143 / SIGTERM):
MinimalPhysicsList::ConstructProcess() was empty - AddTransportation() was
never called. Without transportation, G4SteppingManager cannot compute
geometry-limited step lengths. G4RayTracer geantino particles entered an
infinite zero-step loop, exhausting CI time/memory limits.

Camera placement fix:
/vis/scene/add/volume with no argument added the world volume (10x the solid
size) to the scene; the camera was placed ~36x solid radius away, outside the
world. Geantinos starting outside the world gave an empty navigation history;
G4TouchableHistory crashed on fStackDepth==0. Fix: pass the fixture solid PV
name to /vis/scene/add/volume so scene extent equals the solid, keeping the
camera inside the world.

Cleanup: removed GetRSSKiB, [MEM]/[DBG] prints, RayTracerWarningFilter,
and RayTracerWorkerInitialization (memory investigation artefacts).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: bypass G4TheMTRayTracer to avoid G4AllocatorPool SIGSEGV

G4TheMTRayTracer::CreateBitMap() calls BeamOn(N) which spawns worker
threads that corrupt the G4AllocatorPool<G4TouchableHistory> free-list
(head overwritten with the vptr), causing a SIGSEGV the first time a
worker allocates a G4TouchableHistory.

Instead, instantiate G4TheRayTracer (the single-threaded base class)
directly. Its CreateBitMap() processes events on the master thread via
G4EventManager::ProcessOneEvent() without any worker threads.  The
private Geant4 headers G4TheRayTracer.hh and G4RayTracerViewer.hh
(not installed by the Geant4 build system) are vendored under
src/tests/geometry/geant4_private/ to allow direct instantiation.

Fix geometry-swap crash on subsequent renders:
ReinitializeGeometry(destroyFirst=true) deletes all geometry stores,
but the navigator still holds the old world PV pointer.  The subsequent
call to G4VisManager::GeometryHasChanged() dereferences that dangling
pointer and crashes.  Switch to destroyFirst=false (old geometry
accumulates in stores, acceptable for a test binary) so the old world
PV stays valid through GeometryHasChanged().  After ReinitializeGeometry
sets geometryInitialized=false, call Initialize() again to invoke
Construct() with the new fixture request, then clear the stale scene
models and re-add only the new solid.

All 33 render_geometry_fixtures tests now pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* fix: vendor missing G4RayTracerViewer.hh and G4TheRayTracer.hh private Geant4 headers

Commit 5d768fb by wdconinc referenced these headers as "vendored" under
src/tests/geometry/geant4_private/ and CMakeLists.txt already adds that
directory to the include path, but the actual files were never committed.
This caused the build to fail:

  fatal error: 'G4RayTracerViewer.hh' file not found

These are private Geant4 headers (source/visualization/RayTracer/include/
private/) not installed by the Geant4 build system. They allow direct
instantiation of G4TheRayTracer (the single-threaded base class) rather
than G4TheMTRayTracer, which crashes with G4AllocatorPool corruption when
its CreateBitMap() spawns worker threads from inside a visualisation context.

The headers are copied verbatim from the Geant4 source with the original
Geant4 license blocks retained. The SPDX CI check is updated to ignore
src/tests/geometry/geant4_private so the check skips these files.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: treat Geant4 kInfinity as no-intersection in TraceRay (#255)

Geant4's kInfinity (CLHEP) is 9.9999e+99, a large but finite double.
std::isinf() returns false for it, so rays where DistanceToIn returns
kInfinity (no intersection) were being treated as valid hits.

This caused SurfaceNormal to be evaluated at kInfinity * direction,
a nonsensical point far outside the solid, producing garbage normals
and false surface-normal mismatches.  For G4TwistedTubs (dphi=120°),
this accounted for all 1381 reported surface-normal failures.

Fix:
- DistanceString: also print "kInfinity" for values >= kInfinity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture (#253)

* fix: correct G4ScaledSolid exit normals in DistanceToOut fixture

G4ScaledSolid::DistanceToOut (Geant4 bug, present through at least 11.4.1)
calls fScale->TransformNormal() on the exit normal returned by the unscaled
base solid.  TransformNormal maps normals from global→local frame, but the
base-solid normal (solNorm) is already in the local (unscaled) frame.  The
correct transform is InverseTransformNormal() (local→global), which applies
the inverse-transpose of the scale matrix (divide each component by its
scale factor).  G4ScaledSolid::SurfaceNormal() uses InverseTransformNormal()
correctly, explaining why SurfaceNormal yields 0 mismatches while
DistanceToOut exit normals show 2039/2048 failures for the non-uniformly
scaled sphere fixture (semi-axes 15×20×5 mm).

Fix in G4OCCT: introduce G4ScaledSolidFixed, a minimal subclass that
overrides DistanceToOut to recompute the exit normal via SurfaceNormal()
at the exit point.  SurfaceNormal() is correctly implemented in Geant4,
so this gives the geometrically correct outward normal without any
modification to the Geant4 source tree.  The fixture builder now constructs
G4ScaledSolidFixed instead of G4ScaledSolid for native-solid comparisons.

Remove the broad G4ScaledSolid xfail in fixture_validation.cc (reason was
stale: 'not implemented yet').  Add a comment explaining the Geant4 bug and
the workaround.  Document the bug and fix strategy in provenance.yaml.

commit.message:
fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation (#249)

* fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation

G4DisplacedSolid(base, nullptr, translation) places the *center* of the
base solid at the displacement vector.  G4Box(halfX, halfY, halfZ) is
centered at the origin, so the displaced solid spans:
  [tx-halfX, tx+halfX] x [ty-halfY, ty+halfY] x [tz-halfZ, tz+halfZ]
= [2, 22] x [-22, 8] x [5, 45]  for translation (12,-7,25) and box (20,30,40).

In DRAWEXE, `box` creates a box with one CORNER at the origin, so the
previous generate.tcl `ttranslate displaced_box 12 -7 25` placed the
CORNER at (12,-7,25), producing a solid spanning (12,-7,25)→(32,23,65)
with center at (22,8,45) — displaced by exactly (halfX, halfY, halfZ) =
(10,15,20) from the Geant4 solid center (12,-7,25).

Fix: translate by (tx-halfX, ty-halfY, tz-halfZ) = (2,-22,5) so the
center of the STEP box aligns with the Geant4 displaced solid center.

Regenerate shape.step; volume is unchanged at 20*30*40 = 24000 mm³.

commit.message:
fix(viewer): fix lazy loading deadlock and missing error detail in point cloud viewer (#247)

* Initial plan

* fix(viewer): fix lazy loading deadlock and surface error details

- Replace getWriter/write/close/releaseLock with Blob.stream().pipeThrough()
  in decodeFixtureBlob to avoid a backpressure deadlock when the decompressed
  output of a gzip blob exceeds the DecompressionStream's internal queue size
  (~64 KB).  The old approach wrote all compressed bytes before consuming the
  readable side; for larger point clouds this caused await writer.write() or
  await writer.close() to hang forever, so no points were loaded and no error
  was ever shown.

- In the catch block of selectFixtureById, append a new stats-table row
  containing the full error message (String(err)) when showFixtureMeta has
  already pre-populated the table.  Previously only the text "Error" was placed
  in the hit-count cells, giving the user no indication of why loading failed.

the entire reflect operation. This prevents reflect calls from hanging

perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector with direct ray-plane test for planar faces (#233)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector with direct ray-plane test for planar faces

For faces that are planar and have all-straight-line edges, precompute the
UV polygon at construction time (ComputeBounds) and use a direct ray-plane
intersection + 2D point-in-polygon test instead of IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform.

IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform dominated the B4c callgrind profile at
~1052M instructions (50% of total): the actual line-plane intersection
(IntAna_IntConicQuad) was only ~18M self-instructions; the rest was
overhead from TopExp_Explorer wire traversal, BRepTopAdaptor_FClass2d /
CSLib_Class2d 2D containment, NCollection_Sequence heap alloc/free, and
GeomAdaptor reconstruction on every call.

The fast path:
- RayPlaneFaceHit(): explicit dot-product ray-plane intersection, then
  ElSLib::PlaneParameters to get UV, then ray-casting PointInPolygon2d.
- PointOnPolygonBoundary2d(): detects UV hits within tolerance of any
  polygon edge, triggering the same degenerateRay fallback as TopAbs_ON
  does in the OCCT intersector path (preserves kSurface classification).
- FaceBounds gains uvPolygon (precomputed outer-wire vertices in UV space)
  and outwardNormal (constant outward normal for planar faces, eliminating
  TryGetOutwardNormal / BRepLProp_SLProps calls in DistanceToOut).

Applied in Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), and DistanceToOut(p,v,...).
Non-planar faces and planar faces with curved edges fall back to the
existing IntCurvesFace_Intersector path unchanged.

commit.message:
fix: correct G4VTwistedFaceted generator and TryFindClosestFace robustness (#227)

* fix: correct G4VTwistedFaceted generator and TryFindClosestFace robustness

Three bugs in MakeGenericTwistedFaceted() are fixed:
1. Alpha shear was not applied to the bottom/top cross-section vertices.
   The ±dx1/±dx2 half-widths at y = ∓dy must include a ∓dy·tan(alpha)
   shift so that the top and bottom faces are sheared consistently with
   G4VTwistedFaceted::Inside().
2. The tilt (theta/phi) offset used t·shift (ranging 0→shift) instead
   of (t−0.5)·shift (ranging −shift/2→+shift/2), displacing the solid
   2.6 mm in x and 1.0 mm in y relative to the Geant4 origin.
3. A B-spline loft was used for lateral faces; G4VTwistedFaceted uses
   exact ruled surfaces.  Replace with MakeRuledLoftSolid() (64-section
   piecewise-bilinear ruled loft) consistent with G4TwistedTubs generator.

These bugs caused the regenerated STEP solid to differ significantly from
the Geant4 analytical solid, with the old provenance volume (14383.3 mm³)
being wrong.  Correct value from G4 formula:
  V = ((dx1+dx2+dx3+dx4)*(dy1+dy2) + (dx4+dx3-dx2-dx1)*(dy2-dy1)/3)*dz
    = 14746.667 mm³
64-section ruled-loft OCCT approximation: 14746.516 mm³ (Δ = 0.151 mm³).

Regenerate shape.step and update expected_volume_mm3 in provenance.yaml
and manifest.yaml accordingly.

Core library fix: replace Extrema_ExtPS with BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape
in TryFindClosestFace().  Extrema_ExtPS only minimises the distance on
the unbounded parametric surface and misses boundary minima; for bilinear
(degree-1 B-spline) faces produced by the ruled loft it returns spurious
interior solutions that are closer than the AABB lower bound — a
geometrically impossible result that triggered lb-violation test failures.
BRepExtrema_DistShapeShape considers the bounded face (all edges and
vertices) and always returns the true global minimum.

Mark G4VTwistedFaceted ray-distance mismatches as expected failures
because G4VTwistedFaceted::DistanceToIn/DistanceToOut have known
systematic inaccuracies (errors up to ~5 mm for these parameters).

commit.message:
fix: correct triangular_facet winding in G4TessellatedSolid fixture (#228)

Facets [0,1,2] and [2,3,0] produced inward-pointing normals:
- [0,1,2]: (0,0,0)→(20,0,0)→(0,30,0) gave normal +Z (z=0 face needs -Z outward)
- [2,3,0]: (0,30,0)→(0,0,40)→(0,0,0) gave normal +X (x=0 face needs -X outward)

With wrong normals, G4TessellatedSolid::Inside(centroid=(5,7.5,10)) returned
kOutside instead of kInside, so FixtureComparisonOrigin fell back to the
bounding-box centre (10,15,20) for the native solid.  The imported G4OCCTSolid
correctly classified the centroid as kInside, so the two solids used different
ray origins, causing all 2048 ray comparisons to fail.

Corrected orderings (verified analytically: (B-A)×(C-A) dot (face_centre - centroid) > 0):
- [0,2,1]  was [0,1,2]
- [0,1,3]  unchanged
- [1,2,3]  unchanged
- [2,0,3]  was [2,3,0]

commit.message:
perf: replace BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier in Inside() with ray-parity test (#221)

* perf: replace BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier in Inside() with ray-parity test

Replace the expensive BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform call in
G4OCCTSolid::Inside() with a cheaper ray-parity test using the
already-cached per-face IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

From the B4c callgrind profile, BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform
costs 31.6% of total instructions (3,545M / 11,216M) due to repeated
TopExp_Explorer traversals. The per-face intersector cache avoids this
overhead by casting a single +Z ray and counting face crossings.

Algorithm:
- Cast a ray from p in the +Z direction
- Count intersections where TopAbs_IN (strictly interior to face)
- Odd crossings = kInside, even = kOutside
- Skip TopAbs_ON hits to avoid double-counting shared edges
- If |w| <= tolerance, return kSurface (point on face)
- Fall back to BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier when crossings == 0
  (degenerate ray through edge/vertex) to preserve correctness

The classifier, ClassifierCache, and GetOrCreateClassifier() are
retained as they are still used by ExactDistanceToIn and the fallback
path.

commit.message:
perf: replace BVH traversal with analytical plane-distance safety for all-planar solids (#222)

* perf: replace BVH traversal with plane-distance safety for all-planar solids

For solids where every face is planar (e.g. G4Box, arbitrary polyhedra
with flat faces), DistanceToOut(p) previously performed a full BVH
triangle-mesh traversal (BVH_Traverse::Select) that cost ~15% of total
instructions in B4c callgrind profiles.

This commit introduces an O(N_faces) analytical plane-distance path:

* FaceBounds gains a precomputed std::optional<gp_Pln> for each face.
  Populated in ComputeBounds() by calling BRepAdaptor_Surface::Plane()
  when GetType() == GeomAbs_Plane.

* fAllFacesPlanar (bool) is set once in ComputeBounds() via std::all_of.

* PlanarFaceLowerBoundDistance(p) iterates fFaceBoundsCache and returns
  min_i(plane_i.Distance(p)) — the perpendicular distance to the nearest
  supporting plane.  This is:
  - exact for interior points of convex all-planar solids (e.g. G4Box),
    because the perpendicular foot always lands inside the bounded face;
  - a valid conservative lower bound for non-convex all-planar solids.

* DistanceToOut(p) takes the fast path when fAllFacesPlanar is true,
  avoiding BVHLowerBoundDistance entirely.  For mixed-topology solids
  (some curved faces) the BVH path is preserved unchanged.

No new test failures introduced; all pre-existing failures remain the
same.

commit.message:
fix: correct G4TwistedTubs STEP fixture geometry (#220)

* fix: correct G4TwistedTubs STEP fixture geometry

Two fundamental errors in the G4TwistedTubs STEP generator produced a
fixture (dphi210-phi30-v1) with 56% DTI/DTO ray mismatches and missing
point clouds.  This commit fixes both errors and replaces the fixture.

Root cause 1 — constant cross-sections instead of hyperboloidal surfaces:
  G4TwistedTubs uses G4TwistTubsHypeSide for inner/outer walls; the
  radius varies with z as:
    r(z) = r_end × sqrt(cos²(φ_twist/2) + sin²(φ_twist/2) × (z/halfZ)²)
  At z = 0 (waist): r_inner = 6×cos(15°) = 5.796 mm (not 6 mm).
  The old STEP used constant circular cross-sections (r = 6/12 mm
  everywhere), producing ~5% volume error and wildly wrong geometry.
  Fix: MakeTwistedTubsWire() now computes the correct hyperbolic scale
  factor and passes it to each cross-section wire.

Root cause 2 — phi-range folding for dphi = 210° > 180°:
  When dphi > 180°, the G4TwistTubsSide phi boundaries wrap back on
  themselves.  For dphi=210°, phitwist=30° the effective interior phi
  span at z=0 is only 150°, not 210°.  The old STEP lofted a 210° sector
  producing a completely wrong shape with no valid interior.
  Fix: use dphi = 120° so that dphi/2 = 60° > phitwist = 30° and no
  folding occurs.  The new fixture is tubs-r6-r12-z20-dphi120-phi30-v1.

The new STEP solid is a ruled loft (64 cross-section wires) using the
correct hyperboloidal inner/outer surfaces and 120° phi sector.  OCCT
reports volume = 4321.864 mm³, matching G4's analytical formula to six
significant figures.

Remaining 0.3 mm distance_tolerance_mm:
  After fixing the geometry, 13/2048 rays (0.6%) still show DTI deltas
  of 0.08–0.25 mm.  All 13 are rays nearly tangent to the twisted
  phi-boundary (G4TwistTubsSide) surface.  Analytical verification shows
  OCCT finds the true geometric intersection exactly (delta_phi = 0.000°
  at the hit point); G4's Newton-Raphson solver converges to a spurious
  early root 0.08–0.25 mm before the true surface.  The 0.3 mm tolerance
  covers this known G4 solver inaccuracy rather than any STEP error.

commit.message:
Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p) (#197)

* Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p)

Every DistanceToOut(p) call already returns a provably inscribed sphere
B(p, d) where d is the BVH lower-bound safety distance. Cache these spheres
per-thread and use them to short-circuit Inside(p) before the expensive
BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform call.

Changes:
- Add InscribedSphere{centre, radius} and SphereCacheData structs.
- Seed fInitialSpheres at construction from up to 15 interior candidate
  points (AABB centre, 6 face midpoints, 8 octant centres) using a local
  BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for inside-check and BVHLowerBoundDistance
  for the radius. Sorted descending by radius.
- Add G4Cache<SphereCacheData> fSphereCache (thread-local, same pattern
  as fClassifierCache). GetOrInitSphereCache() copies fInitialSpheres on
  first access per thread or after a shape update.
- TryInsertSphere(centre, d): accepts a new sphere only if it is not fully
  dominated by an existing entry (dominance check avoids sqrt via squaring),
  and the cache is bounded to kMaxInscribedSpheres=64 entries sorted
  descending by radius.
- Inside(p): after the AABB early-out, scan the sphere cache before calling
  OCCT. A hit returns kInside immediately (~640 flops worst case vs the
  ~88 000 flops of BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for a 6-face box).
- DistanceToOut(p): feeds TryInsertSphere after computing the result so
  coverage grows automatically during tracking.
- Destructor: clears fSphereCache.Get().spheres alongside the existing
  classifier/intersector reset.

For g4box-box-20x30x40-v1 the AABB centre seed sphere has radius ≈10 mm,
immediately covering 17% of the interior. After a few dozen tracking steps
the cache reaches near-100% hit rate and the BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier is
never called for interior points.

No new correctness risk: every cached sphere radius d satisfies
d ≤ dist_to_surface(centre) by construction, so no false kInside can occur.

commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop + bbox prefilter (#215)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop

Replace the single IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector::Perform call (33.5%
of total instructions in the B4c callgrind profile, 5,591M instructions)
with a custom per-face loop over IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

Two sources of overhead are eliminated:

1. NCollection_Sequence heap management (~31% of total): the ShapeIntersector
   accumulates results in a heap-allocated NCollection_Sequence (ClearSeq,
   Find, AppendPoint, malloc/free).  The per-face loop instead tracks the
   closest hit in a stack-allocated scalar.

2. All-face intersection without prefiltering (~2%): per-face intersectors
   are gated behind Bnd_Box::IsOut(gp_Lin), rejecting faces whose AABB the
   ray misses in O(1).

Implementation details:

- IntersectorCache now holds std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IntCurvesFace_Intersector>>
  (one per face, heap-allocated to avoid movability requirements) and
  std::vector<Bnd_Box> expandedBoxes (each face box enlarged by
  IntersectionTolerance() to prevent IsOut false positives for zero-thickness
  planar face AABBs).

- GetOrCreateIntersector() rebuilds both vectors on generation mismatch.

- DistanceToOut no longer needs the fFaceAdaptorIndex hash-map lookup:
  the loop tracks minFaceIdx directly, giving O(1) access to the cached
  BRepAdaptor_Surface for normal computation.

- DistanceToOut normal condition updated to accept TopAbs_IN || TopAbs_ON,
  fixing pre-existing failures for sphere/cylinder seam hit points that
  IntCurvesFace_Intersector correctly classifies as ON (not IN).

Smart mouse mode: defer disable until click above viewport (#328)

In smart mode, mouse capture was disabled whenever the mouse moved above
the viewport — too aggressive, preventing hover/movement above the
prompt without losing capture. The intended trigger is user intent to
interact with terminal content above (e.g. text selection), which only a
click signals.

## Changes

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: In the smart mode `on_mouse` handler, gate the
`disable()` call on `MouseEventKind::Down(_)`. Events above the viewport
still return `false` (skipped by flyline) but no longer tear down
capture on mere movement.

```rust
if mouse.row < self.last_viewport_top {
    // Only disable on an actual click above the viewport
    if matches!(mouse.kind, MouseEventKind::Down(_)) {
        self.mouse_state.disable("smart mode: click above the viewport");
    }
    self.last_mouse_over_cell = None;
    return false;
}
```

- **`src/settings.rs`** / **`README.md`**: Updated `MouseMode::Smart`
doc and both README occurrences — "mouse moves above the viewport" →
"user clicks above the viewport".

G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query (#212)

* G4OCCTSolid: reuse cached BRepAdaptor_Surface in DistanceToOut normal query

TryGetOutwardNormal was constructing BRepAdaptor_Surface(face) on every
call from DistanceToOut(p,v,...).  In the B4c profile this accumulates to
1,682M instructions (10.1% of total) across 246k calls.

FaceBounds already stores a BRepAdaptor_Surface adaptor per face, built
once in ComputeBounds().  Add a primary TryGetOutwardNormal overload that
takes a const BRepAdaptor_Surface& directly (moving the existing body
there, minus the adaptor construction).  Keep the original TopoDS_Face
overload as a one-line shim for the SurfaceNormal path (~84 calls).

In DistanceToOut(p,v,...) look up intersector.Face(minIndex) in
fFaceBoundsCache via TopoDS_Shape::IsSame() (O(1) TShape pointer
compare) and pass the cached adaptor to the new overload.  A fallback
to on-the-fly construction is retained for any face not found in the
cache (should not occur in practice).

Expected impact: ~10% reduction in instruction count for workloads
dominated by DistanceToOut(p,v) normal queries (B4c calorimeter example).
